quick_facts.label: Status and meaning
quick_facts.value: “Agniveer” is a distinct four-year rank/engagement under Agnipath. After completing it, an individual may apply for enrolment in the regular cadre; selection is centrally considered against organisational requirements and performance-based objective criteria, with up to 25% of a specific batch eligible to be enrolled. Those selected are to serve a further minimum engagement of 15 years under the applicable JCO/Other Rank service conditions.

quick_facts.label: Terminology caution
quick_facts.value: “Sepoy” is best treated as a regular Army Other Rank discovery label, not as a guarantee that every retained Agniveer receives that exact trade/rank title. Current recruiting communications separately identify regular-cadre entries such as Sepoy Pharma, Nursing Assistant and JCO Religious Teacher, while listing most general soldier intakes as Agniveer categories.

quick_facts.label: 2026 recruiting context
quick_facts.value: For recruiting year 2026–27, the Army publicised a Common Entrance Examination (CEE) process for both Agnipath and regular-cadre recruitment. In the cited regional notice, registration ran from February 13 to April 1, 2026 and the CEE was expected in June; a later official release reported a June 1–12 CEE in Chhattisgarh and subsequent physical, document-verification and medical stages for qualified candidates. Dates, locations and vacancies are cycle-specific.

quick_facts.label: Education alongside the parent route
quick_facts.value: IGNOU currently offers skill-based bachelor’s programmes exclusively for Agniveers of the three Defence Services. Its B.A. (Applied Skills) is a 120-credit programme: 60 credits are delivered by IGNOU and 60 through Defence Services in-service skill training; eligibility is 10+2 or equivalent. This is an education opportunity during the Agniveer stage, rather than an automatic benefit created by regular-cadre selection.

quick_facts.label: Compensation context
quick_facts.value: A person enrolled into the regular cadre is governed by the extant service terms for Indian Army JCOs/Other Ranks rather than the four-year Agniveer package. Fixed salary figures should be checked against current official pay orders: the Eighth Central Pay Commission was constituted on November 3, 2025, so pay-policy context may change.

sections.heading: How this regular-service outcome is reached
paragraphs: The principal route represented by this node is retention from the immediate parent, Agniveer (Indian Army). Agniveers serve under a four-year engagement and, on completion, may be offered the opportunity to apply for permanent enrolment. The Armed Forces consider applications centrally, using objective criteria that include performance over the four-year period and the organisation’s requirements; the stated ceiling is up to 25% of each specific batch. Selection is therefore competitive and is not automatic at the end of four years.

The selected individual’s further engagement is stated as a minimum of 15 years and is subject to the then-applicable terms and conditions for Army JCOs/Other Ranks. The official material does not support treating “Sepoy” as a universal automatic post-retention title: regular-cadre recruitment also includes specialised entries, and the exact stream, trade, rank and conditions must be confirmed in the applicable Join Indian Army notification.

There are also direct regular-cadre recruitment streams in particular specialist categories. For example, 2025 and 2026 Army recruiting releases list Sepoy Pharma alongside Nursing Assistant and JCO Religious Teacher as regular-cadre entries. The 2026 regional CEE notices describe a process in which qualifying candidates proceed to physical fitness and measurement tests, document verification and medical examination. Requirements, recruitment regions, dates and category availability vary by notification.

Applicants should use the official Join Indian Army portal and the relevant recruiting-year notification as the controlling source for eligibility, age, educational credentials, physical standards, medical standards, documents, fees and rally schedule. A February 2026 regional notice reported a ₹250 CEE fee and an April 1, 2026 application deadline for that particular cycle; those figures are historical, regional/cycle-specific context rather than standing requirements.
bullets: Agniveer retention: complete the four-year engagement, then compete for regular-cadre enrolment under the Army’s notified criteria.
Specialist regular-cadre recruitment: monitor current notifications for entries expressly advertised as regular cadre, such as Sepoy Pharma where offered.
Selection sequence in the cited 2026 regional process: online CEE → physical fitness/measurement stages → document verification → medical examination.

sections.heading: Skills developed and assessed
paragraphs: The Government’s Agnipath description states that service imparts military skills and experience, discipline, physical fitness, leadership qualities, courage and patriotism. For a candidate seeking selection into the regular cadre after Agniveer service, sustained performance in these service-relevant areas matters because performance during the four-year engagement is explicitly part of the central consideration for retention.

For specialist regular-cadre pathways, occupation-specific capability is also material. The Army’s recruiting releases distinguish categories such as Sepoy Pharma and Nursing Assistant from general Agniveer categories, so applicants should not assume that generic soldier preparation substitutes for the notified educational or professional requirements of a specialist entry.
bullets: Military discipline, teamwork and physical readiness.
Demonstrated performance throughout the Agniveer engagement, not merely end-of-tenure eligibility.
Leadership and courage developed through service training.
Trade-specific academic or technical preparation where the regular-cadre notification requires it.

sections.heading: Role scope and service responsibility
paragraphs: A regular-cadre Sepoy/Other Rank serves under the service conditions applicable to Army JCOs and Other Ranks. Public recruiting material shows that responsibilities are not uniform across all personnel using the Sepoy label: regular-cadre specialist routes can include Sepoy Pharma, while other listed roles include Nursing Assistant and JCO Religious Teacher. Actual duties consequently depend on the allotted arm, corps, trade, unit and posting, rather than on this node title alone.

The public Agnipath policy material frames the training and service contribution around military skills, discipline, physical fitness, leadership and readiness to support national security in external threats, internal threats and natural disasters. Specific operational duties, unit assignments and deployment details are not appropriately inferred from a recruitment label.
bullets: Serve under applicable Indian Army JCO/Other Rank terms after regular-cadre enrolment.
Perform duties assigned for the individual’s notified and allotted trade or specialist category.
Maintain the military standards developed through training and assessed through service performance.

sections.heading: Compensation and benefits context
paragraphs: Do not use the Agniveer four-year package as the pay description for this node. The Cabinet-approved Agnipath materials state that personnel selected for regular-cadre enrolment move to the existing terms and conditions for Indian Army JCOs/Other Ranks and serve a further minimum engagement of 15 years. Exact basic pay, military service pay, allowances, insurance, pension eligibility and deductions depend on the current rank, trade, location, service rules and government pay orders.

This is particularly important when comparing dated salary information. The Government’s Eighth Central Pay Commission website states that the Commission was constituted on November 3, 2025. Accordingly, a career guide should direct users to current Ministry of Defence, Department of Expenditure and Army notices rather than presenting legacy 7th-CPC figures as a current salary promise.
bullets: Regular-cadre terms apply after selection; the four-year Agniveer package is not the ongoing regular-cadre compensation model.
Allowances and benefits can vary by appointment, duty conditions and policy.
Verify any salary or pension amount against the latest authoritative order before relying on it.
